detail: It was warm to the touch.
The surface was covered in fine scratches.
It seemed older than the town itself.
Someone had written a number on the back.
The edges were worn smooth by handling.
It fit perfectly in the palm of a hand.
There were teeth marks on one edge.
It left a faint residue on the fingers.
The color shifted depending on the angle.
sensory_detail: salt air that shouldn't be here

templates.template: A small {object} was found near the {building}. {detail}
Someone left a {object} on the steps of the {building}. {detail}
A {object} appeared in the {building} overnight. No one remembers bringing it. {detail}
A faint signal came through the Wired last night — just a fragment: "{fragment}"
Static on the Wired resolved briefly into words: "{fragment}"
A data packet arrived at the {building} with no return address. Inside: "{fragment}"
Someone left a note pinned to the {building} door: "{fragment}"
Written in condensation on the {building} window this morning: "{fragment}"
A message was found scratched into the wall of the {building}: "{fragment}"
The {building} smelled different this morning. Like {sensory_detail}.
The lights in the {building} flickered in a pattern for several minutes. Then stopped.
A door in the {building} that was always locked was found open this morning. There was nothing inside but the smell of {sensory_detail}.
The air near the {building} tasted like {sensory_detail} for about an hour.
Footprints near the {building} that don't belong to anyone in town. They lead to the {building2} and stop.
A chair in the {building} was still warm when the first person arrived this morning.
Someone drew a circle in the dust on the {building} floor. Inside it: a {object}.
For a few minutes around {time}, the sky above the {building} looked wrong — darker than it should have been.
It rained on the {building} and nowhere else for exactly four minutes at {time}.
At {time}, shadows near the {building} pointed the wrong direction.
A sound no one could place drifted from the direction of the {building} — like a voice saying: "{fragment}"
A low hum came from underneath the {building} at {time}. It lasted three minutes.
Music was heard near the {building}. No one could identify the instrument.
Someone mentioned seeing "{fragment}" in a dream last night.
Two people in town dreamed about the {building} last night. Neither had been there recently.
A dream image keeps surfacing: a {object} resting on {sensory_detail}.
The Wired dropped a fragment into the town's local network: "{fragment}"
A search query appeared in the {building}'s terminal that no one typed: "{fragment}"
For a moment, every screen in the {building} displayed the same text: "{fragment}"
Something is missing from the {building}. No one can say what.
The {building} was completely silent for eleven minutes at {time}. No hum, no creak, nothing.
A note appeared on the {building} bulletin board this morning: "Is forgetting a form of mercy, or a kind of violence?"
Someone carved a question into the bench outside the {building}: "What do you know now that you couldn't have been told?"
A visitor left a folded paper in the {building}. Inside it said only: "What would you refuse to remember, even if you could?"
Graffiti appeared on the wall near the {building} overnight: "The town remembers what you've forgotten."
A handwritten sign was found near the {building}: "If you could send one message to everyone in town, what would it say?"
A page torn from a book was blowing around near the {building}. The only legible line: "{fragment}"
Someone chalked a question on the ground outside the {building}: "What are you becoming?"
A card was left on the {building} doorstep with neat handwriting: "Name something you believe that no one else in this town believes."
